column1: # Phase 1 — macro foundation. A queued macro of literal chars +
# `~` (Enter) drives the existing handle_key dispatcher exactly as
# if the user had typed the same sequence.
# Run a simple macro that types `hello` and commits with `~`.
# After commit: A1 holds the label, pointer stays at A1 (Enter
# does not move per Lotus), mode returns to READY.
# Sanity: a second macro fed in fresh re-enters LABEL/VALUE per
# the first-char rule. 123~ enters VALUE and commits a number.
